audit_items.proof_trace: CloneKernelInfo() is called three times (lines 2336, 2341, 2346) and its return value is passed directly to RotateKernelInfo() (lines 2337, 2342, 2347) without any NULL check. CloneKernelInfo() returns NULL on allocation failure (AcquireMagickMemory or AcquireAlignedMemory), and RotateKernelInfo() immediately dereferences kernel->next at line 4231, causing a NULL pointer dereference crash. Other call sites in the same file (lines 3769–3770, 4154–4155) correctly check for NULL before use, confirming this is an oversight.
audit_items.attack_scenario: An attacker submits a crafted image to a service using ImageMagick with a kernel specification containing the '<' modifier (mirror expansion), triggering ExpandMirrorKernelInfo(); under attacker-induced memory pressure (e.g., via a large image allocation), CloneKernelInfo() returns NULL and RotateKernelInfo() dereferences it, crashing the process (DoS).
